Then the most human turn — he begs pardon for every past familiarity, for 'O Krishna, O friend' spoken in play and at meals, not knowing this greatness — and pleads to see again the gentle four-armed form, for he cannot bear this one.

verses.devanagari:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तं
इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव ।
तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन
सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते॥
verses.transliteration:
kirīṭinaṃ gadinaṃ cakrahastaṃ icchāmi tvāṃ draṣṭumahaṃ tathaiva
tenaiva rūpeṇa caturbhujena sahasrabāho bhava viśvamūrte
verses.essence: Crowned, with mace and discus in hand — thus I wish to see you, just as before. Take again that four-armed shape, O thousand-armed, O form of all!
verses.translation: I desire to see Thee as before, crowned, bearing a mace, with the discus in hand, in Thy former form only, having four arms, O thousand-armed, Cosmic Form (Being).

anvaya.meaning: crowned, with mace, discus in hand

anvaya.word: इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुम् अहं तथा एव
anvaya.meaning: I wish to see you, just so (as before)
with that very four-armed form
O thousand-armed one, become (that), O all-formed

verses.explanation: The negotiation is touching in its precision: not even the two-armed charioteer yet, but the caturbhuja — Vishnu's four-armed majesty, divinity at a scale a devotee can stand before. Arjuna addresses the thousand-armed and asks it to consent to four. Worship, it turns out, needs a form sized for love.
verses.connection: The step-down request: from the all-form to the adorable form — fulfilled across 11.47–51.
verses.reflection: It is allowed to ask the infinite for a form your love can stand before — ask today.
verses.background: Arjuna, trembling and prostrating, breaks into the Gita's great hymn: salutations in front, behind, on every side, O All. Then the most human turn — he begs pardon for every past familiarity, for 'O Krishna, O friend' spoken in play and at meals, not knowing this greatness — and pleads to see again the gentle four-armed form, for he cannot bear this one.

verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात् ।
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम्॥
verses.transliteration:
mayā prasannena tavārjunedaṃ rūpaṃ paraṃ darśitamātmayogāt
tejomayaṃ viśvamanantamādyaṃ yanme tvadanyena na dṛṣṭapūrvam
verses.essence: The Blessed Lord says: by my grace, Arjuna, this supreme form was shown to you through my own yoga — made of light, universal, endless, primal — which none before you has ever seen.
verses.translation: O Arjuna, this Cosmic Form has graciously been shown to thee by Me by My own Yogic power; full of splendour, primeval, and infinite, this Cosmic Form of Mine has never been seen before by anyone other than thyself.

anvaya.meaning: by me, being gracious, to you, Arjuna, this
supreme form was shown, by my own yoga
made of splendor, universal, endless, primal
which, other than you, none has seen before

verses.explanation: Krishna gently corrects the frame before granting the retreat: this was prasannena — given out of being *pleased*, a grace, not an ordeal — and tvad-anyena na dṛṣṭa-pūrvam: you are the first. The terror Arjuna wants withdrawn was also the rarest gift ever given; both things are allowed to be true.
verses.connection: Names what 11.9–31 was: unprecedented grace — before honoring the request to veil it.
verses.reflection: Reframe one past ordeal tonight as it may truly have been: a rare grace, fiercely wrapped.
verses.background: Krishna gathers the vision back: this form none have seen before by Veda, sacrifice, or austerity — be not afraid, be not bewildered; behold again My familiar shape. Sanjaya reports the mercy: the Lord shows His gentle human form once more and consoles the terrified archer.

verses.devanagari:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानैर्-
न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः ।
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके
द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर॥
verses.transliteration:
na vedayajñādhyayanairna dānaira- na ca kriyābhirna tapobhirugraiḥ
evaṃrūpaḥ śakya ahaṃ nṛloke draṣṭuṃ tvadanyena kurupravīra
verses.essence: Not by Vedas or sacrifices or study, not by gifts, not by rites nor by fierce austerities can I be seen in this form in the world of men by anyone but you, Kuru-hero.
verses.translation: Neither by the study of the Vedas and sacrifices, nor by gifts nor by rituals nor by severe austerities can I be seen in this form in the world of men by any other than thyself, O great hero of the Kurus (Arjuna).

anvaya.meaning: not by Veda-sacrifice-study, not by gifts

anvaya.word: न च क्रियाभिः न तपोभिः उग्रैः
anvaya.meaning: not by rites, nor by fierce austerities
in such a form can I be seen, in the world of men
by any other than you, O hero of the Kurus

verses.explanation: The entire religious toolkit is inventoried and set aside: scripture, sacrifice, study, charity, ritual, austerity — none of it purchases this sight. The vision has no price because it was never for sale; it travels only the channel named in 11.47: grace, answering love. The hardest teaching for the spiritually industrious.
verses.connection: Generalizes 11.47: the vision's currency is relationship, not accomplishment — restated at 11.53–54.
verses.reflection: Stop trying to purchase the unpurchasable today — no rite or merit buys the vision; love is the channel.
verses.background: Krishna gathers the vision back: this form none have seen before by Veda, sacrifice, or austerity — be not afraid, be not bewildered; behold again My familiar shape. Sanjaya reports the mercy: the Lord shows His gentle human form once more and consoles the terrified archer.

verses.devanagari:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो
दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम् ।
व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं
तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य॥
verses.transliteration:
mā te vyathā mā ca vimūḍhabhāvo dṛṣṭvā rūpaṃ ghoramīdṛṅmamedam
vyapetabhīḥ prītamanāḥ punastvaṃ tadeva me rūpamidaṃ prapaśya
verses.essence: Be no more afraid, no more bewildered, having seen this terrible form of mine. With fear gone and heart glad, behold once again this my familiar form.
verses.translation: Be not afraid, nor bewildered on seeing such a teriible form of Mine as this; with thy fear dispelled and with a gladdened heart, now behold again this former form of Mine.

anvaya.word: मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावः
anvaya.meaning: let there be no distress of yours, no bewilderment

anvaya.word: दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरम् ईदृक् मम इदम्
anvaya.meaning: having seen such a terrible form of mine as this
with fear departed, glad-minded, again you

anvaya.word: तत् एव मे रूपम् इदं प्रपश्य
anvaya.meaning: behold this very (former) form of mine

verses.explanation: The consolation is given as a series of releases: mā vyathā, no more anguish — the word that has tolled since the worlds first trembled; vyapeta-bhīḥ, fear dismissed; prīta-manāḥ, gladness restored. The Infinite, having shown what it is, now takes responsibility for the showing's wounds. Revelation ends, as it began, in kindness.
verses.connection: The healing words that accompany the form's withdrawal in 11.50.
verses.reflection: After any overwhelming truth, accept the consolation too: be no more afraid; gladness is also commanded.
verses.background: Krishna gathers the vision back: this form none have seen before by Veda, sacrifice, or austerity — be not afraid, be not bewildered; behold again My familiar shape. Sanjaya reports the mercy: the Lord shows His gentle human form once more and consoles the terrified archer.

verses.speaker: सञ्जय उवाच
verses.devanagari:
इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा
स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः ।
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं
भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा॥
verses.transliteration:
ityarjunaṃ vāsudevastathoktvā svakaṃ rūpaṃ darśayāmāsa bhūyaḥ
āśvāsayāmāsa ca bhītamenaṃ bhūtvā punaḥ saumyavapurmahātmā
verses.essence: Sanjaya says: having spoken thus to Arjuna, Vasudeva showed his own form again; the great Self, becoming once more the gentle-bodied one, comforted him in his terror.
verses.translation: Having thus spoken to Arjuna, Krishna again showed His own form and the great Soul (Krishna), assuming His gentle form, consoled him (Arjuna) who was terrified.

anvaya.meaning: thus having spoken to Arjuna, Vasudeva
showed his own form again
and consoled this terrified one
becoming again gentle-bodied, the great Self

verses.explanation: Two words carry the verse: svakaṁ rūpam — his *own* form, as if the universal blaze were the costume and the dark-skinned charioteer the truth — and saumya-vapuḥ, the gentle body, moonlight after the thousand suns. The Infinite folds itself back into the friend-sized shape, for one reason only: āśvāsayāmāsa, to console.
verses.connection: The visual resolution: the all-form sheathed, the beloved form restored.
verses.reflection: Watch for the gentle form's return after hard revelations — the universe also knows how to console.
verses.background: Krishna gathers the vision back: this form none have seen before by Veda, sacrifice, or austerity — be not afraid, be not bewildered; behold again My familiar shape. Sanjaya reports the mercy: the Lord shows His gentle human form once more and consoles the terrified archer.

verses.speaker: अर्जुन उवाच
verses.devanagari:
दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन ।
इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः॥
verses.transliteration:
dṛṣṭvedaṃ mānuṣaṃ rūpaṃ tava saumyaṃ janārdana
idānīmasmi saṃvṛttaḥ sacetāḥ prakṛtiṃ gataḥ
verses.essence: Arjuna says: seeing this gentle human form of yours, Janardana, I am now composed — restored to my own nature.
verses.translation: Having seen this Thy gentle human form, O Krishna, now I am composed and am restored to my own nature.

anvaya.meaning: seeing this human form
of yours, gentle, O Janardana
now I have become composed
right-minded, returned to my nature

verses.explanation: The exhale of the whole chapter: mānuṣaṁ rūpam — the *human* form, and at the sight of it, prakṛtiṁ gataḥ, Arjuna comes back to himself. The verse quietly crowns the incarnation: the form in which God is most bearable, most lovable, most healing, is the one that looks like us. The thousand suns informed him; the human face restored him.
verses.connection: Closure of the vision-arc — before Krishna draws its doctrine in 11.52–55.
verses.reflection: Notice what restores you to yourself — for Arjuna it was a human face. Keep yours near the shaken.
verses.background: Arjuna breathes — 'seeing this human form of Yours, I am composed and restored to my own nature' — and Krishna seals the chapter's meaning: that form is rarely seen, and not by Vedas, austerity, gifts, or rites, but by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered. The seed-verse follows: work for Me, love Me, cling to nothing, hate no being.

verses.devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच ।
सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम ।
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः॥
verses.transliteration:
sudurdarśamidaṃ rūpaṃ dṛṣṭavānasi yanmama
devā apyasya rūpasya nityaṃ darśanakāṅkṣiṇaḥ
verses.essence: The Blessed Lord says: this form of mine which you have seen is exceedingly hard to see. Even the gods are forever longing for the sight of this form.
verses.translation: Very hard indeed it is to see this form of Mine which thou hast seen. Even the gods are ever longing to behold it.

anvaya.meaning: very hard to see is this form
which you have seen, of mine
even the gods, of this form
are ever desirous of the sight

verses.explanation: A gentle correction of perspective: the vision Arjuna begged to have *ended* is the one heaven stands in line for — devā api... nityaṁ darśana-kāṅkṣiṇaḥ, the gods themselves wait forever for what he was given between two armies on a workday. Grace is often recognized only in the rearview.
verses.connection: Begins the epilogue-teaching: what the vision was worth, and what alone obtains it.
verses.reflection: Treasure today one ordinary access to the sacred that, unknown to you, the gods envy.
verses.background: Arjuna breathes — 'seeing this human form of Yours, I am composed and restored to my own nature' — and Krishna seals the chapter's meaning: that form is rarely seen, and not by Vedas, austerity, gifts, or rites, but by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered. The seed-verse follows: work for Me, love Me, cling to nothing, hate no being.

verses.devanagari:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया ।
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा॥
verses.transliteration:
nāhaṃ vedairna tapasā na dānena na cejyayā
śakya evaṃvidho draṣṭuṃ dṛṣṭavānasi māṃ yathā
verses.essence: Not by the Vedas, not by austerity, not by gifts nor by sacrifice can I be seen in the way you have seen me.
verses.translation: Neither by the Vedas nor by austerity, nor by gift, nor by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as thou hast seen Me (so easily).

anvaya.meaning: not I, by the Vedas, nor by austerity
not by giving, nor by sacrifice
can be seen in such a manner
as you have seen me

verses.explanation: The four pillars of religious merit — study, tapas, charity, ritual — are dismissed a second time, now in the first person and with finality. The repetition from 11.48 is the teaching method: what is said twice at the summit of revelation is meant to be unforgettable. The accounting model of spirituality ends here.
verses.connection: Clears the ground completely for 11.54's single exception.
verses.reflection: Release the spiritual accounting once more: not Vedas, tapas, gifts, nor rites — stop counting today.
verses.background: Arjuna breathes — 'seeing this human form of Yours, I am composed and restored to my own nature' — and Krishna seals the chapter's meaning: that form is rarely seen, and not by Vedas, austerity, gifts, or rites, but by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered. The seed-verse follows: work for Me, love Me, cling to nothing, hate no being.

verses.devanagari:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन ।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप॥
verses.transliteration:
bhaktyā tvananyayā śakya ahamevaṃvidho'rjuna
jñātuṃ draṣṭuṃ ca tattvena praveṣṭuṃ ca parantapa
verses.essence: But by devotion that is undivided, Arjuna, I can in this way be known and truly seen — and entered into, O scorcher of foes.
verses.translation: But by single-minded devotion can I, of this Form, be known and seen in reality and also entered into, O Arjuna.

anvaya.meaning: but by undivided devotion (I) can
I, in such a manner, O Arjuna
be known, and seen in truth
and entered into, O Parantapa

verses.explanation: The single key, after every other door was sealed: bhaktyā tv ananyayā — devotion with no second object, love that does not look sideways. And the three verbs climb like a staircase: jñātum, to know; draṣṭum, to see; praveṣṭum — to *enter*. Beyond information, beyond even vision, lies entry: the lover taken into the beloved. It is the chapter's final answer to 'who are you?' — the one whom love alone can reach all the way. Bhaktya tv ananyaya shakyah — by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered: the vision-chapter's deposited conclusion. The three infinitives ascend — jnatum, drashtum, praveshtum — and Ramanuja's school reads the last as full communion, Shankara as the knowledge that ends otherness. Both schools agree the verse closes every market: after 11.48 and 11.53, love is the one remaining currency.
verses.connection: The doctrine the whole vision was staged to teach — the keystone of the bhakti chapters.
verses.schools_note: Ramanuja's tradition treats this as the Gita's charter: undivided bhakti as the sole sufficient means, with 'praveshtum' read as real communion. Shankara reads ananya-bhakti as devotion-become-knowledge of non-difference. Both agree on the negative half: no quantity of works or learning substitutes.
verses.reflection: Give devotion one undivided hour today — ananya — and aim at all three: know, see, enter.
verses.background: Arjuna breathes — 'seeing this human form of Yours, I am composed and restored to my own nature' — and Krishna seals the chapter's meaning: that form is rarely seen, and not by Vedas, austerity, gifts, or rites, but by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered. The seed-verse follows: work for Me, love Me, cling to nothing, hate no being.

verses.devanagari:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः ।
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव॥
verses.transliteration:
matkarmakṛnmatparamo madbhaktaḥ saṅgavarjitaḥ
nirvairaḥ sarvabhūteṣu yaḥ sa māmeti pāṇḍava
verses.essence: One who does my work, who holds me supreme, devoted to me, freed of attachment, without enmity toward any being — that one comes to me, Pandava.
verses.translation: He who does all actions for Me, who looks upon Me as the Supreme, who is devoted to Me, who is free from attachment, who bears enmity towards no creature, he comes to Me, O Arjuna.

anvaya.meaning: doer of my work, holding me supreme
my devotee, free of attachment
without enmity toward all beings
who (is such) — that one comes to me, O Pandava

verses.explanation: The chapter of overwhelming vision ends in five quiet credentials, fully livable on an ordinary Tuesday: work as his work, him as the highest, love, unclutched hands — and the last, placed where emphasis lives: nirvairaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu, enemy to no being. The man told by Time to slaughter an army is given, as his final instruction, universal friendlessness toward none. Tradition calls this verse the seed of the whole Gita. Mat-karma-krin mat-paramo — the verse Shankara's commentary calls the essence of the whole Gita: His work, His supremacy, His love, no attachment, no enmity toward any being. The tradition notes where the sentence ends: nirvairah sarva-bhuteshu — having shown Arjuna all-devouring Time, the chapter's final requirement is universal friendliness. The vision's last lesson is tenderness.
verses.connection: The chapter's distilled commandment — vision translated into a way of living, bridging into chapter 12's question.
verses.reflection: Live the seed-verse today: His work, His supremacy, His love, no clinging — and enemy to no being.
verses.background: Arjuna breathes — 'seeing this human form of Yours, I am composed and restored to my own nature' — and Krishna seals the chapter's meaning: that form is rarely seen, and not by Vedas, austerity, gifts, or rites, but by undivided devotion alone can I be known, seen, and entered. The seed-verse follows: work for Me, love Me, cling to nothing, hate no being.
